title: stylidium osculum
intro: Stylidium osculum, also known as the trigger plant, is a small, flowering plant native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically for treating respiratory issues such as colds and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The trigger plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the trigger plant typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
